data.title: Anand Rathi Wealth Q2 net profit rises reports 32% YoY to Rs 76 cr, firm declares Rs 7 interim dividend
data.description:
For H1FY2025, the net profit was Rs 150 crore, up 35 percent YoY, while the total revenue during same period grew 35 percent YoY to Rs 495 crore.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Anand Rathi Wealth Limited reported a consolidated net profit of Rs 76 crore, up 32 percent YoY for the quarter ended September 30, 2024. Revenue for the quarter also increased 32 percent for the same period on an annual basis.

For the quarter, the company reported that mutual fund distribution revenue increased 70 percent YoY to Rs 195 crore. Net inflows for the same period grew  128 percent YoY to Rs 5,700 crore.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The Board of Directors declared an interim dividend of Rs 7 per equity share, representing 140% of the face value of Rs 5 each for the company.

For H1FY2025, the net profit was Rs 150 crore, up 35 percent YoY, while the total revenue during same period grew 35 percent YoY to Rs 495 crore.

On the Q2 performance, Rakesh Rawal, Chief Executive Officer noted that India’s growth story is being celebrated as the global economy faces geopolitical challenges and slow growth. "As the fastest-growing large economy, India is projected to achieve a 7% GDP growth for FY25, driven by strong domestic demand, government reforms and increased capital expenditure, which has also boosted its market capitalization. In light of such strong economic growth, the company’s prospects are positive, supported by the rising HNI population who are seeking a strategic approach to wealth creation,” he said.

data.title: Buying a Tata company share is not just an investment, it is an emotion for many long-time investors
data.description:
The sentimental and emotional part aside, Tata shares have also proved to be great investment avenues as is evident from many who have held onto the shares for long and, interestingly, still remember the old names of the Tata companies.

Mumbai-based Farrokh Jijina inherited a number of Tata group company shares in the 90s from his late father and uncle. He believes many of those shares were bought in the Initial Public Offering (IPO) stage.

“It was a foregone conclusion that we Parsis would buy Tata company shares and would never sell them,” says Jijina, who was on his way to NCPA to pay his respects to the late Ratan Tata, where his mortal remains are kept before it is taken away for the final rites.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Jijina himself bought shares of Trent a few months ago and is pleased with the growth in its price over the past 1-year period.

“I still remember, we used to look forward to the coupons of Taj Hotels that used to come to us, the shareholders of Indian Hotels Co Ltd. Then, we could have lunch at the Shamiana (the popular restaurant at the Taj Mahal Towers, Mumbai)," said Jijina, a Zoroastrian community chronicler and a senior editor at Parsiana, a community magazine.

Jijina’s is not an isolated case. There are many like Jijina for whom investing in a Tata company was not just an investment to make money but it was an emotion that they wanted to live with and, more importantly, pass on to the next generation.

While one may believe that it was a trend more prominent among the Parsi community, the fact is that shareholders across age, gender, caste and community flocked to Tata group companies and even the annual general meetings (AGMs) had their fair share of interesting anecdotes, especially those related to the late Ratan Tata.

Also Read: His legacy will continue to inspire generations: IT and Tech honchos pay tribute to Ratan Tata

In one particular instance way back in 2018, well-known investor and a regular face at AGMs, Ashalata Maheshwari told Tata that she would stop attending the AGMs of Tata Group companies the day he gives up his chairmanship of the diversified conglomerate.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

In another instance, Maheshwari, who held shares of hundreds of companies with her portfolio value running into thousands of crores, famously referred to Tata as “hamare handsome chairman.”

While one may look back at these instances in jest, it only shows the affection and the emotion that shareholders had for Tata Group companies, especially when Tata was at the helm – he was the chairman from 1991 to 2012.

The sentimental and emotional part aside, Tata shares have also proved to be great investment avenues as is evident from many who have held onto the shares for long and, interestingly, still remember the old names of the Tata companies.

Also Read: Ratan Naval Tata: The man who transformed a domestic conglomerate into a global player

Godrej Sachinwalla, who is now retired after spending a career at Godrej & Boyce, is not the typical sentimental investor in the Tata group of companies. But as an avid stock market investor and observer, he has been quick to spot opportunities.

He has held shares of several Tata Group companies, including Tata Motors which he still calls TELCO, its earlier name. At present, Sachinwalla holds shares of TCS and Tata Steel.

“TCS is one of the best software companies. It has appreciated well and has a good track record of paying dividends,” says Sachinwalla who claims to have made a tidy fortune on this counter. Aside from TCS, he still holds shares of Tata Steel. “The business is cyclical so the share price oscillates. But its potential is good and is an asset-based company,” he added.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Anlon Healthcare files papers with SEBI to raise funds via IPO
data.description:
Anlon Healthcare

Pharmaceutical intermediates manufacturer Anlon Healthcare has filed preliminary papers with the capital markets regulator SEBI to raise funds via initial public offering.

The IPO will entirely be a fresh issue of 1.4 crore equity shares by the company, with no offer-for-sale component, as per the draft papers filed on October 9.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The company intends to spend Rs 30.7 crore out of the net issue proceeds for the expansion of manufacturing facility, Rs 5 crore for repaying debt, and Rs 35.98 crore for working capital requirements. The remainining funds will be utilised for inorganic acquisitions and general corporate purposes, it said.

With a single manufacturing facility and four R&D labs, the Gujarat-based company is promoted by Punitkumar Rasadia, and Meet Atulkumar Vachhani. Promoters own 70.26 percent stake in the company and the remaining 29.74 percent shares are owned by public shareholders.

Also read: Biggest IPOs of India: Did they help investors make money?

The chemical manufacturing company manufactures advance pharmaceutical intermediates (used as a raw material in active pharmaceutical ingredients), and active pharmaceutical ingredients (API). The APIs are used as a raw material for pharmaceutical formulations in making finished dosage formula (such as tablet, capsules, ointment, syrup etc), ingredients in nutraceuticals formulations, personal care and animal health products.

It is one of the few manufacturers of loxoprofen sodium dihydrate, the API which is widely used in treatment of rheumatoid arthritis, osteoarthritis, lower back pain, frozen shoulder, neck-shoulder-arm syndrome, tooth pain or after surgery, injury or tooth extraction.

Its product portfolio consists of 65 commercialised products, 28 products which are at pilot stage and 49 products which are at R&D stage.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad
Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Also read: Hyundai Motor India IPO GMP crashes 75% in 2 weeks as automaker announces details of mega public issue

Interactive Financial Services is the sole merchant banker to the issue, while KFin Technologies is the registrar to the offer.

Indian Renewable Energy Development Agency (IREDA) on Thursday posted a 36 percent year-on-year (YoY) increase in net profit for the second quarter ended September 30, 2024 to Rs 387.75 crore. It had reported a net profit of Rs 284.73 crore during the July-September period of preceding 2023-24 fiscal, the company said in an exchange filing.

In the June quarter, it reported net profit of Rs 383.69 crore. The total income increased to Rs 1,630.38 crore in the reporting period from Rs 1176.96 crore a year ago.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

IREDA share price settled at Rs 232.70 per share on the NSE, surging 0.68 percent.

data.title: Technical View: Nifty remains directionless, needs to give decisive close above 50 DEMA to kickstart upward rally
data.description:
Nifty Trend

The Nifty 50 closed its volatile and directionless trade moderately higher on October 10, after failing to sustain above the 50-day EMA (Exponential Moving Average - 25,050) for another session. A strong upward rally seems possible only if the index achieves a strong close above this level; until then, consolidation may continue with support in the 24,900-24,800 zone. If it surpasses the 50-day EMA, the 25,250-25,300 range is the next level to watch, according to experts.

After opening higher at 25,067, the Nifty 50 hit an intraday high of 25,134 and a low of 24,979. The index finished just below the 25,000 mark for another session, rising 16.5 points to 24,998.5, forming a small bearish candle with a small upper shadow on the daily charts. This indicates range-bound action around the 24,950-25,150 levels.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The formation of long upper shadows suggests the emergence of weakness near resistance levels. "The index has been failing to reclaim the 50-day EMA for the last two days, indicating prevailing weakness," said Rupak De, Senior Technical Analyst at LKP Securities.

According to Nagaraj Shetti, Senior Technical Research Analyst at HDFC Securities, the double bottom formation in the Nifty around 24,700 is still not confirmed, as the market is not sustaining follow-through upside bounces. A decline below the recent swing low of 24,700 could signal bearish lower top confirmation at 25,230 for the short term, potentially changing the underlying trend to negative.

If the Nifty manages to sustain above the resistance levels of 25,250-25,300 in the next 1-2 sessions, a fresh round of upward movement could resume, potentially pulling the Nifty towards the next hurdles of 25,500-25,600 in the near term.

On the weekly F&O expiry session, the maximum Call open interest was seen at the 25,000 strike, followed by the 25,100 and 25,500 strikes. Maximum Call writing was also at the 25,000 strike, followed by the 25,100 and 25,600 strikes. On the Put side, the 25,000 strike holds the maximum open interest, followed by the 24,500 and 24,900 strikes, with maximum writing at the 25,000 strike, then at 24,900 and 24,500 strikes.

The options data indicated that the index may remain within the range of 24,500-25,500 in the short term, with 25,000 appearing to be a crucial level for direction going forward. The immediate resistance is at 25,100, while support is at the 24,900 level.

Bank Nifty

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The Bank Nifty outperformed the benchmark Nifty 50, closing above 51,500, which is the immediate hurdle on the higher side. The index rallied 524 points, or 1.03 percent, to 51,531, backed by private banks, and formed a bullish candlestick pattern on the daily charts with low volume, approaching the 50-day EMA (51,777).

The index is still trading within the range of the strong bearish candle made on October 7 (51,784-50,194). As long as it holds below the 51,750 zone, weakness could be seen towards 51,000, then 50,750 levels. On the upside, resistance is observed at 51,750 and then 52,000 levels, according to Chandan Taparia, Senior Vice President | Analyst-Derivatives at Motilal Oswal Financial Services.

Volatility declined further, gradually making the trend favourable for bulls. The India VIX, the fear index, fell 4.44 percent to 13.50, continuing its downtrend for the third consecutive session.

data.title: Domestic investors continue buying despite heavy FII selling in recent sessions
data.description:
Most market experts continue to remain positive on the stability of the Indian market on the back of strong support from domestic investors, highlighting that India's dependency on FIIs has reduced over time.

Domestic investors, both institutional and retail, have been demonstrating their growing influence in the Indian stock markets for quite some time even as their foreign counterparts have been selling shares in huge quantity in the last few trading sessions.

This has not gone unnoticed by a large section of the market. In a post on social media platform X, Abhisar Jain, a fund manager at Monarch AIF shared data related to the flows between September 30 and October 4 and said, “All the punches thrown by FIIs are being countered by Domestic Investors....This vote of confidence is truly remarkable!”

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

FPIs have been aggressively offloading stake, especially on days of extreme market volatility. Between September 30 and October 4, FIIs sold shares worth around Rs 39,964 crore, with the most significant selling witnessed on October 3, when they were net sellers at Rs 14,854 crore, according to data shared by Monarch AIF.

Domestic investors, particularly mutual funds, however, counterbalanced this with strong buying as mutual funds were net buyers at Rs 27,893 crore.

Other categories of domestic investors also showed confidence in the market despite external selling pressures. During the period, they picked up shares worth around Rs 11,633 crore. Insurance companies also contributed to the buying, with net purchases totalling Rs 1,859 crore.

Banks and Portfolio Management Services (PMS) were also net buyers, picking up shares worth Rs 723 crore and Rs 169 crore, respectively.

In a recent conversation with Moneycontrol, Nirav Karkera, Head of Research, Fisdom had said that the DIIs’ ability to absorb the selling pressure reflects their confidence.

“They are not waiting for deeper discounts, they are buying at current levels, even as FIIs sell. This shows conviction and liquidity on the domestic front, which helps stabilise the market in the face of external shocks,” he said.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

In a comment to the media in September, Venkat Chalasani, the AMFI CEO, had noted that as mutual funds remain a key stabilising factor for the equity market in India, their commitment towards transparency and excellence remains resolute.

Most market experts continue to remain positive on the stability of the Indian market on the back of strong support from domestic investors, highlighting that India's dependency on FIIs has reduced over time.

Kotak AMCs Harsha Upadhyaya believes that fifteen years ago, India was heavily dependent on foreign flows but that has changed now.

“If something had gone wrong, foreign investors would have completely moved out of India, shifting their focus to markets like China, which had a much larger weightage," Upadhyaya said while adding that today India is second only to China in weightage within the MSCI Emerging Markets Index and this makes it difficult for foreign investors to ignore India entirely.

While there continues to be bouts of FPI selling, on an overall basis FIIs continue to find India attractive. In September, FIIs were net buyers at $6.9 billion – the highest monthly net flows since December 2023. In October, however, they have been net sellers at nearly $6 billion till October 9.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: Tata Sons to earn nearly Rs 2,600-crore income as TCS announces 2nd interim dividend for FY25
data.description:
TCS Q2 Results 2024 Live Updates: All eyes are on whether TCS will match the stellar growth reported by rival Accenture, which saw a surge in revenue driven by Gen AI projects.

Tata Sons Pvt Ltd stands to earn about Rs 2,600 crore in dividend income from its shareholding in India's largest IT services exporter Tata Consultancy Services Ltd (TCS).

Tata Sons held 71.74 percent or 259 crore shares of the IT bellwether at the end of June 2024 quarter, according to company filings with the stock exchanges. Tata Investment Corporation held 0.03 percent stake in the company as of June 2024 quarter.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

TCS declared an interim dividend of Rs 10 per share in Q2FY25 (July-September). Earlier, the company paid an interim dividend of Rs 10 per share on July 19, 2024 for the financial year 2024-25 (FY25). Prior to that, a final dividend of Rs 28 was paid by the company to its shareholders on May 16, 2024.

TCS Dividend 2024 Record Date

TCS has already fixed Friday, October 18, 2024 as the record date to determine the eligibility of shareholders for the corporate action.

TCS Dividend History

Ex-DateAmount (Rs)
18 Oct, 202410
19 July, 202410
16 May, 202428
19 Jan, 20249
19 Jan, 202418

TCS has a dividend yield of 1.32 percent at the current levels. It's stock price settled at Rs 4,227.90 per share on the NSE on October 10, down marginally by 0.59 percent.

TCS shares advanced 10.96 percent in 2024 so far, while the benchmark Sensex rose 12.92 in the same period.

data.title: TCS Q2 FY25 net profit falls to Rs 11,909 crore, misses estimates; dividend declared
data.description:
TCS declares fiscal second quarter financial results.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) posted a 1.1 percent quarter-on-quarter (QoQ) fall in net profit for the second quarter ended September to Rs 11,909 crore, missing Street expectations. India’s largest IT service firm’s Q2 FY24 revenue from operations rose to Rs 64,259 crore, showed the stock exchange filing.

A Moneycontrol poll of 10 brokerages pegged TCS’ July-September net profit at Rs 12,420 crore, and revenue at Rs 63,938 crore.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

TCS also declared the second interim dividend of Rs 10 per share. The IT bellwether has already fixed Friday, October 18, 2024 as the record date to determine the eligibility of shareholders for the corporate action. Earlier, the company paid an interim dividend of Rs 10 on 19 July 2024.

Revenue growth was led by Energy, Resources and Utilities sectors, said the company in a statement.

"We saw the cautious trends of the last few quarters continue to play out in this quarter as well. Amidst an uncertain geopolitical situation, our biggest vertical, BFSI showed signs of recovery. We also saw a strong performance in our Growth Markets," said K Krithivasan, Chief Executive Officer and Managing Director of TCS.

The IT major's Q2 operating margin was at 24.1 percent, while the net margin was at 18.5 percent. TCS added a net 5,726 employees during the quarter.

Analysts had expected the Tata group IT giant’s Q2 revenue to be driven by the ramp-up of key deals, particularly its ongoing partnership with BSNL, and the net profit to be bolstered by robust deal execution and the growth in North America, BFSI, and retail.

TCS second quarter earnings come amid scaling of its Rs 15,000 crore BSNL deal. The deal involves establishing data centres and 4G sites across India, and laying the foundation for future 5G infrastructure.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad
Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Ahead of the results, TCS share price fell 0.6 percent today to end at Rs 4227.9 on NSE.
data.author: MoneyControl
